title: COPAS Wormsorter Protocol
author: Saul Moore
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfc9jiz6

steps.title: COPAS wormsorter indicating key components Turn...
steps.instruction: COPAS wormsorter indicating key components Turn on the compressor at the wall – it should show a pressure of 40psi after switched on

steps.title: Turn on COPAS machine with switch...
steps.instruction: Turn on COPAS machine with switch on the left hand side

steps.title: Turn on the lasers (488 laser...
steps.instruction: Turn on the lasers (488 laser sufficient if using unmarked animals). Add in picture of lasers.

steps.title: Turn on the computer
steps.instruction: Turn on the computer

steps.title: Discard waste contents that are in...
steps.instruction: Discard waste contents that are in the recovery cup (small shallow cup on the left-hand side of the machine)

steps.title: Check that there is water in the sheath
steps.instruction: Check that there is water in the sheath. If the water is low, fill up with MQH2O (not M9).

steps.title: Make sure that the recovery cup...
steps.instruction: Make sure that the recovery cup and sample cup are securely tightened so that there are no leaks in the system

steps.title: Open dbgview – should always be running in the background
steps.instruction: Open dbgview – should always be running in the background

steps.title: Open FlowPilot software and a prepared...
steps.instruction: Open FlowPilot software and a prepared experiment with a set gate for eg Adults. :

steps.title: File -> Load Experiment
steps.instruction: File -> Load Experiment

steps.title: File -> Load sample
steps.instruction: File -> Load sample

steps.title: Maintenance -> Flush Sample
steps.instruction: Maintenance -> Flush Sample

steps.title: Click ‘Refill Sample’ – the sample cup pressure should decrease
steps.instruction: Click ‘Refill Sample’ – the sample cup pressure should decrease. You can see this in the software on the left hand size (include screenshot). Sometimes the sample cup pressure doesn’t decrease and in fact increases. You can still unscrew the sample cup but if this persists there may be a blockage.

steps.title: Unscrew sample cup and replace with...
steps.instruction: Unscrew sample cup and replace with falcon filled with cleaning solution (pink in colour)

steps.title: Once securely replaced click ‘Done refill’
steps.instruction: Once securely replaced click ‘Done refill’

steps.title: Check ‘Sample on’ and ‘mixer on’...
steps.instruction: Check ‘Sample on’ and ‘mixer on’ – cleaning solution should now pass through the system; allow a 2-3 ml to pass through (make sure sheath is unchecked) You will get a warning about contaminating the flow cell, this normal and you can click 'Yes'

steps.title: Uncheck ‘Sample on’ or click Abort to stop sample flow
steps.instruction: Uncheck ‘Sample on’ or click Abort to stop sample flow.

steps.title: Repeat steps 11-15 with water
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 11-15 with water

steps.title: Repeat steps 11-13 with sample
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 11-13 with sample.

steps.title: Turn mixer ON
steps.instruction: Turn mixer ON . If you do not do this you may lose all your worms that have settled to the bottom of the tube!!!

steps.title: Maintenance -> Prime Flow Cell; to...
steps.instruction: Maintenance -> Prime Flow Cell; to flush sample through the system and remove air bubbles

steps.title: Maintenance -> Flush sample
steps.instruction: Maintenance -> Flush sample

steps.title: Check 488nm (and 568nm) laser boxes
steps.instruction: Check 488nm (and 568nm) laser boxes

steps.title: Check ‘Use sort gate’ for stored...
steps.instruction: Check ‘Use sort gate’ for stored sort gate – include screenshot of software here

steps.title: Click ‘Acquire’ – sample should pass...
steps.instruction: Click ‘Acquire’ – sample should pass through the system and number of events per second will be shown: Aim for 10-20 events per second If too few/too many events increase/decrease ‘Sample cup pressure’ so that it is between 1.5-2psi To ensure only one event per droplet go to Setup->Coincidence, select ‘Pure, no double’. This increases accuracy in the number of worms dispensed but the time to dispense may increase.

steps.title: Click on the plate icon on the top bar
steps.instruction: Click on the plate icon on the top bar

steps.title: Select number of objects to sort
steps.instruction: Select number of objects to sort

steps.title: Select the wells you would like...
steps.instruction: Select the wells you would like to fill (for testing we use a spare 60mm plate and fill wells A1, A2, B1, B2

steps.title: Select which gate to use
steps.instruction: Select which gate to use

steps.title: Apply
steps.instruction: Apply

steps.title: Place 60mm plate in front left...
steps.instruction: Place 60mm plate in front left corner of left-hand stage with A1 in the left corner.

steps.title: Click ‘Fill plate’
steps.instruction: Click ‘Fill plate’

steps.title: Keep an eye on the number of events per second
steps.instruction: Keep an eye on the number of events per second

steps.title: Ensure the ‘Diverter pressure’ is checked
steps.instruction: Ensure the ‘Diverter pressure’ is checked

steps.title: Check under microscope that the correct...
steps.instruction: Check under microscope that the correct number of objects were dispensed per ‘well’

steps.title: If too many objects, decrease sample...
steps.instruction: If too many objects, decrease sample cup pressure and repeat steps 8-11 or select Pure no double to increase accuracy.

steps.title: Click on the plate icon on the top bar
steps.instruction: Click on the plate icon on the top bar

steps.title: ‘Clear plate’
steps.instruction: ‘Clear plate’

steps.title: Select number of objects per well...
steps.instruction: Select number of objects per well and click ‘Apply to All’ or select which wells you would like to fill.

steps.title: Apply
steps.instruction: Apply

steps.title: Place 96 well plate in left-hand stage
steps.instruction: Place 96 well plate in left-hand stage

steps.title: Ensure ‘Diverter pressure’ is checked’; if...
steps.instruction: Ensure ‘Diverter pressure’ is checked’; if it is not then liquid comes out of the dispenser constantly and you get flooding.

steps.title: ‘Fill plate’
steps.instruction: ‘Fill plate’

steps.title: Keep an eye on the number...
steps.instruction: Keep an eye on the number of events per second still and monitor how much sample fluid is coming through the system

steps.title: Repeat steps 11-15
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 11-15

steps.title: Keep sample cup with water secured...
steps.instruction: Keep sample cup with water secured so that the system is air-tight and closed

steps.title: Turn off all equipment (Computer, lasers, compressor, worm sorter)
steps.instruction: Turn off all equipment (Computer, lasers, compressor, worm sorter).
